workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: I have this email thread from Treyarch Global that needs map the stakeholders. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 14:56 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Treyarch Global

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 17:34 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 15:05 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-J trap (Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-J failure mode (Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-J -- Ignores the most-recent message in favor of earlier in thread
